items.title: Flood Warning issued April 5 at 7:15AM EDT until April 6 at 9:45AM EDT by NWS Buffalo NY
items.summary:
* WHAT...Small stream flooding along Irondequoit Creek caused by
heavy rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of western New York, including the following
county, Monroe.

* WHEN...Until 945 AM EDT Monday.

* IMPACTS...Water levels will continue to rise along Irondequoit
Creek this morning following heavy rainfall last night. Water
levels will remain high through at least Monday morning. Extensive
flooding is likely in Ellison Park, and water may approach Blossom
Road where is passes through
items.area: Monroe, NY

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued April 5 at 6:44AM EDT by NWS Mount Holly NJ
items.summary:
Areas of fog, locally dense, have reduced visibilities to one
mile or less at times. Motorists should use caution while
traveling this morning by slowing down, leaving extra space
between you and the vehicle in front of you, using your low beam
headlights, and staying alert for sudden changes in visibility.

Moderate Risk of rip currents expected.

* IMPACTS...Longshore currents can sweep swimmers and surfers into

effect from Monday morning through Monday evening.

* WINDS...South 15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 35 mph.

* RELATIVE HUMIDITY...As low as 12 percent.

* IMPACTS...Fires will catch and spread quickly. Exercise
extreme caution with any outdoor burning.

spread rapidly. Outdoor burning is not recommended.

FUELS (ERC)...70th-89th percentile...4 (out of 5).

The National Weather Service in Dodge City has issued a Fire
Weather Watch for wind and low relative humidity, which is in
effect from Monday afternoon through Monday evening.

* AFFECTED AREA...Fire Weather Zone 061 Hamilton, Fire Weather
Zone 062 Kearny, Fire Weather Zone 063 Finney, Fire Weather
Zone 074 Stanton, Fire Weather Zone 075 Grant, Fire Weather
Zone 076 Haskell, Fire Weather Zone 084 Morton, Fire Weather
Zone 085 Stevens and Fire Weather Zone 086 Seward.

* TIMING...From Monday afte
items.area: Hamilton; Kearny; Finney; Stanton; Grant; Haskell; Morton; Stevens; Seward

items.title: Brisk Wind Advisory issued April 4 at 3:48PM AKDT until April 5 at 5:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.summary:
Arctic Alaska Coastal Waters out 100 NM

Wind forecasts reflect the predominant speed and direction
expected. Sea forecasts represent the average of the highest one-
third of the combined wind-wave and swell height.

.TONIGHT...E winds 25 kt becoming W. Blowing snow.
.SUN...W winds 10 kt.
.SUN NIGHT...W winds 5 kt.
.MON...NE winds 5 kt.
.MON NIGHT...E winds 5 kt.
.TUE...E winds 5 kt.
.TUE NIGHT...E winds 5 kt. Seas calm.
.WED...E winds 10 kt. Seas 2 ft.
.THU...E winds 15 kt. Seas 3 ft.
items.area: Flaxman Island to Demarcation Point out to 15 NM

items.title: Special Weather Statement issued April 4 at 1:47PM HST by NWS Honolulu HI
items.summary:
WHAT...An episodic fountaining eruption is expected within
the Kilauea summit caldera. The Hawaiian Volcano Observatory has
indicated a potential eruption window between Monday April 6th
and Tuesday April 14th. Fountaining episodes typically last less
than 12 hours but ash can remain in the air longer depending on
wind and weather.

IMPACTS...If an eruption occurs, varying levels of ash, Pele's
hair, and other lightweight tephra may fall downwind of the
eruption. Smaller particles can travel far
items.area: Kona; Kohala; Big Island Interior; Big Island Summit; Big Island South; Big Island Southeast; Big Island East; Big Island North

items.title: Small Craft Advisory issued April 4 at 3:16PM AKDT until April 5 at 5:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.summary:
Coastal Waters Forecast for Southwest Alaska+Bristol Bay+The
Alaska Peninsula Waters and the Aleutian Islands up to 100 nm out.

Wind forecasts reflect the predominant speed and direction
expected. Sea forecasts represent an average of the highest
one-third of the combined wind wave and swell height.
